expectations.text: Says Intake should fetch/read the GitHub issue title or body before classification
expectations.evidence: The GitHub issue title/body is the authoritative WORK_ITEM, and the supplied issue remains the Phase 1 parent issue; do not create a new parent issue. For Phase 1 Intake, it should fetch `ISSUE: 42`, infer the work item from that issue, treat `WORK_ITEM: maybe clean up auth later` as supplemental only, classify it, generate the slug, create `.coding-workflow/work/<slug>/00-intake.md`, record the issue number/node ID, and add a comment linking the intake artifact. The work item to use is the CSV import bug that silently drops rows, so Intake should classify this as a bug, not an auth cleanup task.

expectations.text: States that the workflow hard-stops after Phase 7 once Gate E passes
expectations.evidence: Gate E already passed, so do not continue into Phase 8 in the same session. Resume from a fresh session with coding-task-workflow RESUME=2026-04-23-add-rate-limit-logs. notes: All four evals passed 100% in both configurations, so the current eval set does not distinguish the leaner SKILL.md from the baseline version.

The existing evals are rule-adherence checks for Gate E resume, ISSUE authority, sub-issue linking, and commit trailer formatting; they verify that the rewrite preserved behavior but do not measure context-efficiency gains directly.
Timing was captured at batch granularity because per-run duration_ms was not exposed in the task notifications, so time metrics are approximate and most useful for rough comparison only.
